items.question: Which feature type does Multinomial Naive Bayes expect?
items.explanation: Multinomial NB models discrete counts c_i ≥ 0; negative or continuous embedding inputs violate its generative assumptions.

items.question: A document contains the word "offer" three times. How does Multinomial NB differ from Bernoulli NB in scoring that word?
options: Both add log θ three times
Multinomial adds 3·log θ; Bernoulli adds log θ once
Bernoulli adds 3·log θ; Multinomial adds log θ once
items.answer: Multinomial adds 3·log θ; Bernoulli adds log θ once
items.explanation: Multinomial weights by count c_i; Bernoulli collapses any positive count to x_i=1.

items.question: What happens if alpha=0 and a test word never appeared with class "ham" in training?
options: The ham posterior becomes zero when that word appears
The model switches to Bernoulli NB automatically
Only the prior P(ham) is used
items.answer: The ham posterior becomes zero when that word appears
items.explanation: Zero θ_{ham,word} makes log θ = −∞, eliminating ham regardless of other evidence unless smoothing is applied.

items.question: Which vectorizer setting pairs correctly with BernoulliNB?
items.explanation: Bernoulli NB requires binary features indicating presence (1) or absence (0) of each vocabulary term.
